steps.description: Click the "App feature" option within the left-side navigation menu in the "Configure" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal dashboard.
Click on the "Messaging extension" option in the "App features" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal.
Click the text input field labeled "Enter a bot ID" in the "Messaging extension" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.

Click the input field labeled "Enter a bot ID" to focus on it for entering or modifying the bot ID within the Messaging extension configuration screen of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal application.
Type text: 'b34bebd8-88a8-4474-9b16-104999a0a869' into the "Enter a bot ID" input field within the "Messaging extension" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Save" button at the bottom-right corner of the "Identify your bot" configuration section in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click on the "App features" option in the left-side navigation menu within the "Configure" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Press the "End" key to scroll to the bottom of the "App features" configuration page in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal.
Click the checkbox labeled "Deselect to keep the original botId value" in the dropdown menu of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu.
Click on the "Default folder" text field within the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or modify the path `/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm the application name entered in the "Input an application name" text box within the Visual Studio Code interface.
